yesterdayAnalysis: Completed a 6.1mi run with TSS 66 at HR 145 — this appears to have been Monday's planned full rest day shifted, or an unscheduled session. Given the plan called for full rest Monday and easy 30min spin Tuesday, this run volume (6.1mi vs today's planned 3mi) is higher than the sharpener phase prescription. The effort level was moderate (HR 145) but acceptable if legs felt ready.
todayRecommendation: Execute today's prescribed 2,000m easy pool swim plus 3mi easy run only if hip and legs feel recovered from yesterday's 6.1mi effort. Keep the run truly easy — this is controlled aerobic work in the sharpener phase, not a tempo effort. Tomorrow's 3x8min race-pace bike intervals are the key stimulus this week.
weekProgress: Week 11 of 13 shows 108 TSS of 340 target (32%) with 4 days remaining including today. This is perfectly on-track given the heavily back-loaded schedule — Saturday's long ride and Thursday's brick intervals will contribute the bulk of weekly TSS. The schedule rhythm is appropriate for the sharpener phase with nothing hard after Wednesday.
keyInsight: Yesterday's 6.1mi run exceeded the prescribed easy volume for this taper phase — monitor hip response closely today and ensure Thursday's race-pace bike intervals aren't compromised by residual fatigue.
